items.headline: City releases children and youth services master plan update
items.summary: San José presented a status report on its Children and Youth Services Master Plan on April 9, including updates on youth empowerment programs and the Safe Summer Initiative. The report covers how the city is investing in programs serving young residents.

items.headline: Mountain View zoning hearing postponed; 333 Franklin Street project delayed
items.summary: The Administrative Zoning hearing scheduled for Wednesday, April 8 was cancelled, pushing the next hearing to Wednesday, April 22. A project at 333 Franklin Street that was on the April 8 agenda will be re-noticed once a new hearing date is set, meaning the project's review timeline has been extended.

items.headline: Street pavement replacement project underway across San José
items.summary: The City Council awarded a contract for the 2026 Local Streets Remove and Replace Pavement Project on April 7, which will repair deteriorating streets throughout San José. Residents should expect construction activity and potential traffic impacts in their neighborhoods.

items.headline: City Updates Active Transportation Plan with New Scoring Criteria
items.summary: Cupertino is revising its Active Transportation Plan (walking, biking, transit infrastructure) with Phase 2 updates and revised evaluation criteria. This plan will shape how the city prioritizes pedestrian and bicycle projects over the coming years.

items.headline: City Council approves construction project with new contract award
items.summary: The council adopted plans and specifications for a construction project and awarded a contract, though specific project details aren't listed in the agenda. Residents may want to follow up on what infrastructure or facility is being built.

items.headline: City considers major update to federal housing and community plan
items.summary: Council reviewed a substantial amendment to the 2025-2030 Consolidated Plan, which affects how federal housing and community development dollars are spent in Milpitas. This could impact affordable housing priorities and community programs over the next five years.

items.headline: Major senior housing complex approved near downtown
items.summary: Trillium senior apartments will add 65 affordable units at 678 E St John St with nearly $8.8M in funding. This addresses San José's critical shortage of senior housing options.

items.headline: City Council adopts emergency interim housing siting policy
items.summary: San José approved a new emergency interim housing siting policy on April 1, which will affect how the city identifies and uses locations for temporary housing solutions. This policy change could impact neighborhood planning and housing availability citywide.
